title: The Eastern Fence Circuit

features.name: corroded-trace
features.desc-on-touch: Property lines oxidize into copper traces. The fence that marked the boundary between private and wild has become the circuit that connects them.

features.name: rust-node
features.desc-on-touch: A junction point where boundaries have begun to dissolve. Rust blooms into signal, decay becomes connection.

features.name: eucalyptus-solder
features.desc-on-touch: Where copper meets wood, something has begun to fuse—property claims melting into the organic code of the scrub.

features.name: boundary-dissolution
features.desc-on-touch: You trace the line where fence becomes circuit, circuit becomes creek-bed. The distinction no longer holds charge.

features.name: eastern-run
features.desc-on-touch: The fence runs east, a copper line now, a circuit path, carrying signals that taste of rust and eucalyptus oil.

inscription: the fence that asked passage or barrier has oxidized into connection; does the question matter after this long

file.name: Property_Boundary_Decay_Spectral_Analysis_1983_Onward.csv
file.note: Eastern run: resistance decreasing. Organic growth in circuits. Archive suspects the scrub is learning to broadcast.

exits.label: back through the gate to the scrub's edge where rust began
further east along the circuit where eucalyptus converges with code
back toward the scrub's interior, where boundaries have become suggestions
